creator: bsd@bontempi.net
text:
I installed 4Pane on a FreeBSD 13.1 desktop with KDE. I have been using FreeBSD since 2002.

Upon first launch the 4Pane window froze for a few seconds. I clicked on the window top bar. The 4Pane window became whitish and was unmovable. A few seconds after that the window became normal again. I noticed a message at the bottom indicating 4Pane was deleting files from my home directory. I tried to quit the program but it wouldn't. I tried to pkill it, and it did not work. Then, I kill -9 it.

I saw countless files inside ~/.cache/4Pane/.Trash/DeletedBy4Pane/.

My home directory was so trashed that upon reboot I could not even start Xorg.

I had to recover all files from backup. I am not installing it ever again of course.

I am submitting this as a heads-up. I am not saying this is purportedly malign app, but it is not safe at all.
